acronyms.usage: Decentralized network model where each node shares resources directly
Centralized file storage device connected to network
High-speed dedicated network for block-level storage
Unix/Linux protocol for distributed file sharing
Windows protocol for file and printer sharing
Protocol for transferring files between systems
Network mechanism to prioritize traffic for resource sharing
Software for managing shared database access

visualTips.visualization: Draw a central server with arrows pointing to multiple client computers around it, showing request-response flow
Draw all computers connected in a mesh (circle or line) with bidirectional arrows showing equal exchange between all nodes
Draw incoming requests as arrows splitting into multiple paths going to different servers, then recombining
Show a remote file icon on one computer appearing as a local folder on another computer with a dotted line connection

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant where customers (clients) order food from the kitchen (server) rather than cooking themselves
Like neighbors sharing gardening tools - each person owns tools and borrows/loans directly without a central tool library
Like multiple checkout counters at a supermarket - customers are directed to less crowded lines to reduce wait time
Like a shared filing cabinet in an office that everyone can access through their desk
Like utilities (electricity, water) - pay for what you use, delivered on-demand without owning generation equipment
